exercises.instruction_pt: Qual é o significado da palavra em inglês?
exercises.passage: Cat
exercises.question: O que significa 'cat'?
options: A) Pássaro
B) Gato
C) Peixe
D) Cachorro
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Cat' = gato.

exercises.instruction_pt: Qual é o significado da palavra em inglês?
exercises.passage: Dog
exercises.question: O que significa 'dog'?
options: A) Coelho
B) Cachorro
C) Gato
D) Rato
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Dog' = cachorro.

exercises.instruction_pt: Qual é o significado da palavra em inglês?
exercises.passage: Book
exercises.question: O que significa 'book'?
options: A) Caneta
B) Mochila
C) Livro
D) Caderno
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Book' = livro.

exercises.instruction_pt: Qual é o significado da palavra em inglês?
exercises.passage: School
exercises.question: O que significa 'school'?
options: A) Igreja
B) Loja
C) Hospital
D) Escola
exercises.explanation_pt: 'School' = escola.

exercises.instruction_pt: Qual é o significado da palavra em inglês?
exercises.passage: Car
exercises.question: O que significa 'car'?
options: A) Ônibus
B) Carro
C) Trem
D) Bicicleta
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Car' = carro.

exercises.instruction_pt: Qual é a palavra em inglês para:
exercises.passage: Água
exercises.question: Como se diz 'água' em inglês?
options: A) Juice
B) Coffee
C) Water
D) Milk
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Water' = água.

exercises.instruction_pt: Qual é a palavra em inglês para:
exercises.passage: Casa
exercises.question: Como se diz 'casa' em inglês?
options: A) Door
B) Room
C) Home
D) House
exercises.explanation_pt: 'House' = casa (o prédio). 'Home' = lar (conceito).

exercises.instruction_pt: Qual é o significado da palavra em inglês?
exercises.passage: Happy
exercises.question: O que significa 'happy'?
options: A) Feliz
B) Bravo
C) Triste
D) Cansado
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Happy' = feliz.

exercises.instruction_pt: Qual é o significado da palavra em inglês?
exercises.passage: Big
exercises.question: O que significa 'big'?
options: A) Pequeno
B) Grande
C) Médio
D) Alto
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Big' = grande.

exercises.instruction_pt: Qual é o significado da palavra em inglês?
exercises.passage: Good
exercises.question: O que significa 'good'?
options: A) Bom
B) Péssimo
C) Ruim
D) Mais ou menos
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Good' = bom.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia a frase e escolha o significado correto:
exercises.passage: I am a student.
exercises.question: O que a frase significa?
options: A) Eu sou médico.
B) Eu sou estudante.
C) Eu sou engenheiro.
D) Eu sou professor.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'I am a student' = Eu sou um(a) estudante.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia a frase e escolha o significado correto:
exercises.passage: She lives in Brazil.
exercises.question: O que a frase significa?
options: A) Ela estuda no Brasil.
B) Ela nasceu no Brasil.
C) Ela mora no Brasil.
D) Ela trabalha no Brasil.
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Lives in' = mora em. 'She lives in Brazil' = Ela mora no Brasil.

exercises.passage: The hospital is next to the bank.
exercises.question: Onde fica o hospital?
options: A) Longe do banco
B) Atrás do banco
C) Em frente ao banco
D) Ao lado do banco
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Next to' = ao lado de. O hospital fica ao lado do banco.

exercises.passage: My mother is cooking dinner.
exercises.question: O que a mãe está fazendo?
options: A) Cozinhando o jantar
B) Lavando roupa
C) Limpando
D) Dormindo
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Cooking dinner' = cozinhando o jantar. 'Mother' = mãe.

exercises.passage: It is raining outside.
exercises.question: Como está o tempo?
options: A) Está sol
B) Está nevando
C) Está ventando
D) Está chovendo
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Raining' = chovendo. 'Outside' = lá fora.

exercises.passage: He has three children.
exercises.question: Quantos filhos ele tem?
options: A) Quatro
B) Um
C) Três
D) Dois
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Three children' = três filhos/crianças.

exercises.passage: The store opens at 9 AM.
exercises.question: A que horas a loja abre?
options: A) 9h
B) 7h
C) 10h
D) 8h
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Opens at 9 AM' = abre às 9 da manhã. 'Store' = loja.

exercises.passage: I want to buy a new phone.
exercises.question: O que a pessoa quer?
options: A) Consertar um telefone
B) Trocar um telefone
C) Vender um telefone
D) Comprar um telefone novo
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Buy' = comprar. 'New phone' = telefone novo.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia a frase e escolha o significado:
exercises.passage: The children are playing in the park.
exercises.question: O que as crianças estão fazendo?
options: A) Comendo no parque
B) Dormindo no parque
C) Brincando no parque
D) Estudando no parque
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Playing' = brincando/jogando. 'Park' = parque.

exercises.passage: We need to go to the airport.
exercises.question: Para onde precisam ir?
options: A) Estação de trem
B) Rodoviária
C) Aeroporto
D) Porto
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Airport' = aeroporto. 'We need to go' = precisamos ir.

exercises.passage: Maria is from São Paulo. She works as a nurse at a hospital.
exercises.question: What is Maria's job?
options: A) Doctor
B) Nurse
C) Lawyer
D) Teacher
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Nurse' = enfermeira. Maria trabalha como enfermeira.

exercises.passage: John wakes up at 6 AM every day. He takes the bus to work.
exercises.question: How does John go to work?
options: A) By car
B) By train
C) On foot
D) By bus
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Takes the bus' = pega o ônibus. John vai de ônibus para o trabalho.

exercises.passage: The restaurant is closed on Mondays. It opens at 11 AM on other days.
exercises.question: When is the restaurant closed?
options: A) On Tuesdays
B) Every day
C) On Mondays
D) On Sundays
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Closed on Mondays' = fechado às segundas-feiras.

exercises.passage: Ana loves animals. She has two dogs and one cat at home.
exercises.question: How many pets does Ana have?
options: A) Three
B) Four
C) One
D) Two
exercises.explanation_pt: 2 cachorros + 1 gato = 3 animais de estimação no total.

exercises.passage: It is very cold today. You should wear a jacket.
exercises.question: What should you wear?
options: A) A jacket
B) Shorts
C) A T-shirt
D) Sandals
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Jacket' = jaqueta/casaco. Está muito frio, então deve usar casaco.

exercises.passage: Pedro studies English every day. His exam is next month.
exercises.question: When is Pedro's exam?
options: A) Next week
B) Next year
C) Today
D) Next month
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Next month' = mês que vem. A prova de Pedro é no mês que vem.

exercises.passage: The supermarket is on Main Street. It is between the bank and the pharmacy.
exercises.question: Where is the supermarket?
options: A) In front of the church
B) Between the bank and the pharmacy
C) Behind the hospital
D) Next to the school
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Between' = entre. O supermercado fica entre o banco e a farmácia.

exercises.passage: Laura is 25 years old. Her brother is 3 years younger.
exercises.question: How old is Laura's brother?
exercises.explanation_pt: Laura tem 25 anos. O irmão é 3 anos mais novo: 25 - 3 = 22 anos.

exercises.passage: The movie starts at 8 PM. We should leave at 7:30.
exercises.question: What time should they leave?
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz 'leave at 7:30' = sair às 19h30.

exercises.passage: Carlos can speak three languages: Portuguese, English, and Spanish. He learned English at school.
exercises.question: Where did Carlos learn English?
options: A) At home
B) At school
C) In another country
D) On the internet
exercises.explanation_pt: 'He learned English at school' = Ele aprendeu inglês na escola.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia o parágrafo e identifique a ideia principal:
exercises.passage: São Paulo is the largest city in Brazil. It has more than 12 million people. The city is known for its restaurants, museums, and busy streets. Many people from different countries live there, making it one of the most diverse cities in the world.
exercises.question: What is the main idea of this paragraph?
options: A) Many tourists visit São Paulo
B) São Paulo has many restaurants
C) São Paulo is the capital of Brazil
D) São Paulo is a large and diverse city
exercises.explanation_pt: O parágrafo descreve São Paulo como a maior cidade do Brasil, com 12 milhões de pessoas e grande diversidade. A ideia principal é que é uma cidade grande e diversa.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia o parágrafo e identifique a ideia principal:
exercises.passage: Drinking water is very important for your health. Doctors recommend drinking at least 2 liters of water per day. Water helps your body stay hydrated, removes toxins, and keeps your skin healthy. Many people don't drink enough water and suffer from headaches and tiredness.
exercises.question: What is the main idea?
options: A) Water is expensive
B) Headaches are common
C) Doctors are important
D) Drinking water is important for health
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto inteiro fala sobre a importância de beber água para a saúde.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia o parágrafo e identifique a ideia principal:
exercises.passage: Learning a new language takes time and practice. You need to study vocabulary, grammar, and pronunciation. The best way to improve is to practice every day, even if it's just for 30 minutes. Watching movies and listening to music in the new language can also help.
exercises.question: What is the main idea?
options: A) Grammar is the most important part of a language
B) Movies are the best way to learn
C) Learning a language requires daily practice
D) You need a teacher to learn a language
exercises.explanation_pt: A ideia central é que aprender um idioma leva tempo e a melhor forma é praticar todo dia.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia o parágrafo e identifique a ideia principal:
exercises.passage: Exercise is good for both your body and your mind. Regular physical activity can reduce stress, improve sleep quality, and help you maintain a healthy weight. Even a simple 30-minute walk every day can make a big difference in your overall health.
exercises.question: What is the main idea?
options: A) Exercise benefits your body and mind
B) Stress is a big problem
C) Walking is boring
D) You need to go to a gym
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto fala dos benefícios do exercício para o corpo e a mente.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia o parágrafo e identifique a ideia principal:
exercises.passage: Recycling is one of the easiest ways to help the environment. When you recycle paper, plastic, and glass, these materials can be used again instead of going to a landfill. Many cities now have recycling programs that make it easy for people to separate their trash.
exercises.question: What is the main idea?
options: A) Cities have too much trash
B) Recycling helps protect the environment
C) Landfills are very large
D) Glass is the easiest material to recycle
exercises.explanation_pt: A ideia principal é que reciclar é uma forma fácil de ajudar o meio ambiente.

exercises.passage: Brazil is famous for its Carnival celebration. Every year, millions of people participate in parades, dance to samba music, and wear colorful costumes. The biggest celebrations take place in Rio de Janeiro and Salvador. Carnival usually happens in February or March.
exercises.question: When does Carnival usually happen?
options: A) February or March
B) March or April
C) December or January
D) January or February
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz 'usually happens in February or March' = geralmente acontece em fevereiro ou março.

exercises.passage: Public transportation in big cities can be very crowded during rush hour. Many people prefer to take the subway because it is faster than the bus. However, the subway doesn't reach all neighborhoods, so some people have no choice but to take the bus or drive.
exercises.question: Why do some people take the bus instead of the subway?
options: A) The bus is cheaper
B) The bus is faster
C) The subway doesn't go to all areas
D) They like buses more
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que o metrô não chega a todos os bairros, então algumas pessoas precisam pegar ônibus.

exercises.passage: Cooking at home is healthier and cheaper than eating out. When you cook your own meals, you can control the ingredients and the amount of salt and sugar. Many young people are learning to cook by watching tutorials on YouTube.
exercises.question: According to the text, how are young people learning to cook?
options: A) At school
B) By watching YouTube tutorials
C) From their parents
D) At restaurants
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que muitos jovens aprendem a cozinhar assistindo tutoriais no YouTube.

exercises.passage: Sleep is essential for good health. Adults need between 7 and 9 hours of sleep per night. Not getting enough sleep can affect your memory, mood, and ability to concentrate. Experts recommend avoiding screens before bedtime to improve sleep quality.
exercises.question: How many hours of sleep do adults need?
options: A) 6 to 8 hours
B) 5 to 7 hours
C) 8 to 10 hours
D) 7 to 9 hours
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz 'between 7 and 9 hours' = entre 7 e 9 horas.

exercises.passage: The internet has changed the way people work. Many companies now allow their employees to work from home. This is called remote work. Remote work can save time because people don't need to commute, but some workers feel lonely without the social interaction of an office.
exercises.question: What is one disadvantage of remote work mentioned in the text?
options: A) Companies don't like it
B) Workers feel lonely
C) The internet is slow
D) It is more expensive
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto menciona que alguns trabalhadores se sentem solitários (lonely) sem a interação social do escritório.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia o texto e responda a questão de detalhe:
exercises.passage: The Amazon rainforest is the largest tropical rainforest in the world. It covers about 5.5 million square kilometers across nine countries, with the majority located in Brazil. The Amazon is home to approximately 10% of all species on Earth, including jaguars, pink river dolphins, and over 40,000 plant species.
exercises.question: What percentage of Earth's species live in the Amazon?
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz 'approximately 10% of all species on Earth' = aproximadamente 10% de todas as espécies da Terra.

exercises.passage: Coffee was first discovered in Ethiopia, Africa, around the 9th century. A goat herder named Kaldi noticed that his goats became very energetic after eating berries from a certain tree. He tried the berries himself and felt more awake. Today, Brazil is the world's largest producer of coffee.
exercises.question: Who discovered coffee according to the story?
options: A) A goat herder in Ethiopia
B) A scientist in Europe
C) A farmer in Brazil
D) A merchant in Arabia
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que um pastor de cabras (goat herder) chamado Kaldi na Etiópia descobriu o café.

exercises.passage: The human body is made up of about 60% water. The brain and heart are composed of 73% water, and the lungs are about 83% water. Even bones contain about 31% water. This is why staying hydrated is so important for every part of your body.
exercises.question: Which organ has the highest water content?
options: A) The bones
B) The heart
C) The lungs
D) The brain
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que os pulmões (lungs) são compostos por 83% de água, o maior percentual mencionado.

exercises.passage: Mount Everest is the highest mountain in the world, standing at 8,849 meters above sea level. It is located on the border between Nepal and Tibet. The first people to reach the summit were Sir Edmund Hillary from New Zealand and Tenzing Norgay from Nepal, in 1953.
exercises.question: In what year was Mount Everest first climbed?
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que Hillary e Norgay chegaram ao topo em 1953.

exercises.passage: Honey never spoils. Archaeologists have found pots of honey in ancient Egyptian tombs that were over 3,000 years old and still perfectly edible. Honey's long shelf life is due to its low moisture content and acidic pH, which create an environment where bacteria cannot grow.
exercises.question: Why does honey last so long?
options: A) Because of its low moisture and acidic pH
B) Because it is stored in tombs
C) Because it is kept in sealed containers
D) Because bees add chemicals to it
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto explica que a longa duração do mel se deve ao baixo teor de umidade (low moisture) e pH ácido (acidic pH).

exercises.passage: The Great Wall of China stretches over 21,000 kilometers. It was built over many centuries by different Chinese dynasties, primarily to protect against invasions from the north. Contrary to popular belief, the Great Wall cannot be seen from space with the naked eye.
exercises.question: What is NOT true about the Great Wall according to the text?
options: A) It can be seen from space
B) It is over 21,000 km long
C) It was built for protection
D) It was built by different dynasties
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que, ao contrário da crença popular, a muralha NÃO pode ser vista do espaço a olho nu.

exercises.passage: Octopuses are considered one of the most intelligent invertebrates. They can solve puzzles, open jars, and even escape from aquariums. Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood. When they feel threatened, they can change color and texture to blend in with their surroundings.
exercises.question: How many hearts does an octopus have?
options: A) Four
B) Two
C) Three
D) One
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz 'three hearts' = três corações.

exercises.passage: The first Olympic Games were held in ancient Greece in 776 BC. Only men were allowed to compete, and the events included running, wrestling, and chariot racing. The modern Olympic Games began in 1896 in Athens, Greece, and now include both men and women from almost every country in the world.
exercises.question: When did the modern Olympics begin?
options: C) 776 BC
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz 'The modern Olympic Games began in 1896' = Os jogos olímpicos modernos começaram em 1896.

exercises.passage: Bananas are the most popular fruit in the world. India is the largest producer, followed by China and Indonesia. Bananas are rich in potassium, vitamin B6, and fiber. Interestingly, bananas are technically classified as berries by botanists, while strawberries are not.
exercises.question: Which country produces the most bananas?
options: A) Indonesia
B) Brazil
C) China
D) India
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz 'India is the largest producer' = a Índia é o maior produtor.

exercises.passage: Leonardo da Vinci was born in Italy in 1452. He is famous for painting the Mona Lisa and The Last Supper. But da Vinci was more than just a painter — he was also a scientist, engineer, and inventor. His notebooks contain designs for flying machines, tanks, and solar power systems, centuries before these inventions became reality.
exercises.question: What was Leonardo da Vinci besides a painter?
options: A) A scientist, engineer, and inventor
B) A musician and poet
C) A doctor and philosopher
D) A king and politician
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que ele também era cientista, engenheiro e inventor.

exercises.passage: Working from home has become very popular since 2020. Many employees enjoy the flexibility of choosing their own schedule and not having to commute. However, some people find it difficult to separate work from personal life. They end up working longer hours because they don't have a clear boundary between the office and home.
exercises.question: What is one disadvantage of working from home mentioned in the text?
options: A) Workers don't have computers
B) People work longer hours due to no clear boundaries
C) Companies don't allow it
D) The internet is too slow
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que as pessoas acabam trabalhando mais horas porque não têm uma divisão clara entre escritório e casa.

exercises.passage: Vegetarian diets are becoming more common around the world. People choose to stop eating meat for different reasons: some do it for health benefits, others for environmental concerns, and many for animal welfare. Studies show that a well-planned vegetarian diet can provide all the nutrients the body needs.
exercises.question: According to the text, why do people become vegetarian?
options: A) Because meat is too expensive
B) Because doctors recommend it
C) For health, environment, and animal welfare
D) Only for health reasons
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto menciona três razões: saúde (health), meio ambiente (environmental concerns) e bem-estar animal (animal welfare).

exercises.passage: Public libraries offer much more than just books. Most modern libraries have free Wi-Fi, computer stations, study rooms, and community programs. Many libraries also offer free workshops on topics like coding, financial literacy, and job interview preparation. For students who don't have a quiet place to study at home, the library is an essential resource.
exercises.question: What can you find in modern libraries besides books?
options: A) Only Wi-Fi
B) Sports equipment
C) Food and drinks
D) Wi-Fi, computers, study rooms, and workshops
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto lista: Wi-Fi grátis, computadores, salas de estudo e workshops gratuitos.

exercises.passage: Getting enough sleep is crucial for university students. Research shows that students who sleep less than six hours per night have lower grades and are more likely to get sick. The brain needs sleep to consolidate memories, which means that studying all night before an exam — known as 'cramming' — is actually less effective than studying earlier and getting a good night's sleep.
exercises.question: Why is 'cramming' less effective than sleeping well before an exam?
options: A) Because exams are easy
B) Because students forget everything anyway
C) Because teachers give extra points for sleeping
D) Because the brain needs sleep to consolidate memories
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto explica que o cérebro precisa de sono para consolidar memórias ('consolidate memories'), tornando estudar a noite toda menos eficaz.

exercises.passage: Social media has changed the way young people communicate. Instead of calling friends on the phone, most teenagers prefer to send text messages or use social media apps. While this makes communication faster and easier, some psychologists worry that young people are losing the ability to have face-to-face conversations and read body language.
exercises.question: What skill are young people potentially losing according to psychologists?
options: A) The ability to read books
B) The ability to write
C) The ability to have face-to-face conversations
D) The ability to use technology
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que psicólogos se preocupam que jovens estão perdendo a habilidade de ter conversas presenciais (face-to-face conversations) e ler linguagem corporal.

exercises.passage: Electric cars are becoming more affordable and popular. The main advantages are lower fuel costs and reduced pollution. However, there are still some challenges: charging stations are not available everywhere, and the batteries can take several hours to fully charge. As technology improves, these problems are expected to decrease.
exercises.question: What is still a challenge for electric cars?
options: A) They are too fast
B) They are too small
C) Limited charging stations and long charging times
D) They use too much gas
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto menciona que postos de carregamento não estão disponíveis em todo lugar e as baterias levam horas para carregar completamente.

exercises.passage: Learning a musical instrument has many benefits beyond just playing music. Studies have shown that children who learn to play an instrument perform better in math and science. Playing music also improves memory, coordination, and the ability to concentrate. Adults who play instruments report lower levels of stress and anxiety.
exercises.question: What academic subjects do children who play instruments perform better in?
options: A) History and geography
B) Art and literature
C) Math and science
D) Physical education
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que crianças que aprendem um instrumento se saem melhor em matemática (math) e ciências (science).

exercises.passage: Fast food is convenient and inexpensive, but eating it regularly can have serious health consequences. Fast food meals are typically high in calories, salt, sugar, and unhealthy fats. Regular consumption has been linked to obesity, heart disease, and type 2 diabetes. Nutritionists recommend limiting fast food to once or twice a month and choosing healthier options when possible.
exercises.question: How often do nutritionists recommend eating fast food?
options: A) Three times a week
B) Once or twice a month
C) Never
D) Every day
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que nutricionistas recomendam limitar fast food a uma ou duas vezes por mês (once or twice a month).

exercises.passage: Volunteering is a great way to give back to your community while also developing new skills. Many universities look favorably at volunteer experience when reviewing applications. Volunteering can also help you discover new interests, make connections, and even improve your mental health. Studies show that people who volunteer regularly report higher levels of happiness and life satisfaction.
exercises.question: According to the text, how can volunteering help with university applications?
options: A) Volunteers don't need to take exams
B) Volunteering replaces grades
C) Universities look favorably at volunteer experience
D) Universities give free tuition to volunteers
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que muitas universidades veem com bons olhos (look favorably) a experiência de voluntariado ao analisar candidaturas.

exercises.passage: Coffee is one of the most consumed beverages in the world, and Brazil is the largest producer. The country produces about one-third of the world's coffee supply. Most Brazilian coffee is grown in the states of Minas Gerais, São Paulo, and Espírito Santo. The tropical climate and altitude of these regions create ideal conditions for growing high-quality coffee beans.
exercises.question: What fraction of the world's coffee does Brazil produce?
options: A) Two thirds
B) One quarter
C) One third
D) One half
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz 'about one-third of the world's coffee supply' = cerca de um terço do café mundial.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia o texto e responda a pergunta de inferência:
exercises.passage: When Maria arrived at the airport, she realized she had left her passport at home. Her flight was in two hours, and her house was forty minutes away. She immediately called her roommate, who was still at home, and asked her to bring the passport to the airport as fast as possible.
exercises.question: What can we infer about Maria's situation?
options: A) She might still catch her flight if her roommate arrives in time
B) She doesn't need a passport
C) She decided not to travel
D) She will definitely miss her flight
exercises.explanation_pt: O voo é em 2 horas e a casa fica a 40 minutos. Se a colega de quarto vier rápido, Maria ainda pode pegar o voo. Não é certeza, mas é possível.

exercises.passage: The restaurant had been open for only three months, but it was already fully booked every night. The chef, who had trained in Paris, created a menu that combined French techniques with Brazilian ingredients. Food critics praised the innovative dishes, and several celebrities had been spotted dining there.
exercises.question: What can we infer about the restaurant?
options: A) It is very cheap
B) It only serves French food
C) It is struggling to attract customers
D) It is very successful and popular
exercises.explanation_pt: O restaurante está lotado todas as noites, recebe elogios de críticos e celebridades frequentam. Podemos inferir que é muito bem-sucedido e popular.

exercises.passage: After years of declining enrollment, the small rural school announced it would close at the end of the semester. Many families had moved to the city in search of better job opportunities, leaving fewer children in the village. The nearest school would now be thirty kilometers away, creating a difficult situation for the remaining families.
exercises.question: What problem will the remaining families face?
options: A) Their children will need to travel far to attend school
B) The village will have too many students
C) They will have to pay higher fees
D) The teachers will lose their jobs
exercises.explanation_pt: Com a escola mais próxima a 30km, as famílias restantes terão que enviar seus filhos para longe para estudar.

exercises.passage: Dr. Santos had been treating patients for over thirty years. Every morning, he arrived at the clinic before anyone else and left after the last patient. His colleagues often joked that he would never retire. Last week, however, he surprised everyone by announcing his retirement, saying he wanted to spend more time with his grandchildren.
exercises.question: What can we infer about Dr. Santos?
options: A) He didn't like his job
B) He was forced to retire
C) He retired because he was sick
D) He was extremely dedicated to his work
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto mostra que Dr. Santos chegava primeiro, saía por último, e colegas brincavam que nunca se aposentaria. Isso indica extrema dedicação ao trabalho.

exercises.passage: The city government installed speed cameras on the main highway last year. Since then, the number of traffic accidents has decreased by 40%. However, many drivers complain about receiving expensive fines for exceeding the speed limit by just a few kilometers per hour. The government says the cameras are about safety, not revenue.
exercises.question: What do some drivers believe about the speed cameras?
options: A) They should be on every street
B) They make driving more fun
C) They are primarily a way to collect money from drivers
D) They are too slow
exercises.explanation_pt: O fato de motoristas reclamarem de multas caras e o governo precisar dizer que 'não é sobre receita' sugere que alguns motoristas acreditam que as câmeras são principalmente para arrecadar dinheiro.

exercises.passage: Scientists have discovered a new species of frog in the Amazon rainforest. The frog is unusual because it is completely transparent — you can see its internal organs through its skin. Researchers believe there may be many more undiscovered species in the Amazon, but deforestation threatens to destroy habitats before scientists can study them.
exercises.question: What concern do researchers express?
options: A) There are too many frogs in the Amazon
B) Species may go extinct before being discovered due to deforestation
C) The frog is dangerous
D) Transparent frogs cannot survive
exercises.explanation_pt: Os pesquisadores temem que o desmatamento destrua habitats antes que cientistas possam estudar espécies que ainda não foram descobertas.

exercises.passage: When smartphones first appeared, many teachers banned them from classrooms, viewing them as distractions. Today, however, some schools are incorporating smartphones into their teaching methods. Students use them to access educational apps, take notes, and even conduct research during class. The debate over phones in schools continues, but the trend is moving toward controlled integration rather than complete prohibition.
exercises.question: What is the current trend regarding phones in schools?
options: A) Schools are moving toward controlled use rather than banning
B) Students prefer not to use phones
C) All schools have banned phones
D) Teachers have given up on teaching
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que a tendência é 'controlled integration rather than complete prohibition' — integração controlada em vez de proibição total.

exercises.passage: The city's new bicycle lane system has been both praised and criticized. Cyclists appreciate the safer routes, and studies show a 25% increase in cycling since the lanes were built. However, car drivers complain that the lanes have reduced road space, causing more traffic congestion. Business owners along the routes report mixed results — some say fewer customers come by car, while others have seen an increase in foot traffic.
exercises.question: What is the effect of bicycle lanes on businesses?
options: A) Businesses are not affected
B) All businesses have fewer customers
C) Results vary — some gain customers, some lose them
D) All businesses have more customers
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz 'mixed results' — resultados variados. Alguns negócios perderam clientes de carro, outros ganharam mais pedestres.

exercises.passage: Japan's population has been declining for over a decade. The birth rate is one of the lowest in the world, and young people are increasingly choosing not to have children, citing financial concerns and career pressures. At the same time, Japan has one of the highest life expectancies. This creates a situation where there are more elderly people than young workers to support them.
exercises.question: What economic problem does Japan face?
options: A) Too many young workers
B) Too many children being born
C) Not enough elderly care facilities
D) Not enough young workers to support the growing elderly population
exercises.explanation_pt: Com poucos nascimentos e alta expectativa de vida, há mais idosos do que jovens trabalhadores para sustentá-los.

exercises.passage: The tiny island nation of Tuvalu in the Pacific Ocean faces an existential threat from rising sea levels. The highest point on the island is only about four meters above sea level. Scientists predict that by 2100, much of Tuvalu could be underwater. The government has already begun negotiating with neighboring countries to accept its citizens as climate refugees.
exercises.question: Why is Tuvalu negotiating with other countries?
options: A) To find new places for its citizens to live if the island floods
B) To build more hotels for tourism
C) To import more food
D) To create a military alliance
exercises.explanation_pt: O governo está negociando para que outros países aceitem seus cidadãos como refugiados climáticos, caso a ilha seja inundada.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia os dois textos e compare:
exercises.passage:
Text A: Online education has grown rapidly in recent years. Students can learn at their own pace, access materials from anywhere, and often pay lower tuition fees. Many prestigious universities now offer complete degree programs online.

Text B: Despite its growth, online education faces criticism. Employers sometimes view online degrees as less valuable than traditional ones. Students also report feeling isolated without face-to-face interaction, and dropout rates for online courses are significantly higher than for in-person classes.
exercises.question: What is one advantage of online education mentioned in Text A that is NOT contradicted in Text B?
options: A) Students never feel isolated
B) Online degrees are valued equally by employers
C) Dropout rates are low
D) Students can learn at their own pace
exercises.explanation_pt: Text A menciona que alunos podem aprender no próprio ritmo. Text B não contradiz isso — as críticas são sobre empregadores, isolamento e desistência, não sobre o ritmo de aprendizado.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia os dois textos e compare:
exercises.passage:
Text A: Renewable energy sources like solar and wind power are essential for combating climate change. They produce no greenhouse gas emissions during operation and are becoming increasingly cost-competitive with fossil fuels.

Text B: While renewable energy has many benefits, it also presents challenges. Solar panels and wind turbines require large areas of land. Energy storage remains expensive, and the supply is intermittent — the sun doesn't always shine, and the wind doesn't always blow.
exercises.question: What challenge of renewable energy is mentioned in Text B?
options: A) It is too expensive to install
B) It causes pollution
C) It produces greenhouse gases
D) Energy supply is not constant
exercises.explanation_pt: Text B menciona que o fornecimento é intermitente (intermittent) — o sol nem sempre brilha e o vento nem sempre sopra.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia os dois textos e compare:
exercises.passage:
Text A: Artificial intelligence is transforming healthcare. AI systems can analyze medical images faster and more accurately than human doctors in some cases. This technology has the potential to improve diagnosis, reduce errors, and save lives.

Text B: However, the use of AI in healthcare raises ethical concerns. Questions about data privacy, algorithmic bias, and accountability arise: if an AI system makes a wrong diagnosis, who is responsible? Additionally, many patients feel uncomfortable being diagnosed by a machine rather than a human doctor.
exercises.question: Both texts would likely agree that:
options: A) AI has benefits but also raises important concerns
B) Patients prefer AI over human doctors
C) AI should replace all doctors
D) AI should never be used in healthcare
exercises.explanation_pt: Text A fala dos benefícios e Text B das preocupações. Ambos concordariam que a IA tem benefícios mas também levanta questões importantes.

exercises.passage:
Text A: Gap years — taking a year off between high school and university — have become increasingly popular among young people. Supporters argue that a gap year provides valuable life experience, helps students discover their interests, and can prevent burnout.

Text B: Critics of gap years point out that students who take time off often struggle to return to academic life. They may lose study habits, fall behind their peers, and face financial challenges. Some research suggests that gap year students have lower first-year university grades compared to those who go directly.
exercises.question: According to Text B, what happens to some students who take gap years?
options: A) They always earn more money
B) They all succeed in university
C) They become better students
D) They may have trouble returning to academic life
exercises.explanation_pt: Text B diz que estudantes que tiram um ano de folga frequentemente têm dificuldade para voltar à vida acadêmica.

exercises.passage:
Text A: Bilingual children — those who grow up speaking two languages — have been shown to have cognitive advantages. Research indicates they are better at multitasking, problem-solving, and focusing attention. They also tend to develop stronger communication skills.

Text B: Some parents worry that raising children bilingually may cause language confusion or developmental delays. However, most linguistic researchers consider these concerns unfounded. While bilingual children may initially mix languages, this is a normal part of bilingual development and typically resolves by age five.
exercises.question: How do most linguistic researchers view parents' concerns about bilingualism?
options: A) They haven't studied the topic
B) They agree that bilingualism causes delays
C) They consider the concerns unfounded
D) They recommend only one language
exercises.explanation_pt: Text B diz que a maioria dos pesquisadores linguísticos considera essas preocupações infundadas (unfounded).

theme: Artigos curtos — múltiplos tipos de questão

exercises.passage: Space tourism is no longer science fiction. In 2021, several private companies successfully launched civilian passengers into space. While a ticket currently costs hundreds of thousands of dollars, industry experts predict that prices will decrease significantly over the next two decades, potentially making space travel accessible to middle-class tourists. However, environmental groups have raised concerns about the carbon footprint of rocket launches.
exercises.question: What is the main idea of this article?
options: A) Everyone can afford space travel now
B) Environmental groups support space tourism
C) Space tourism is becoming reality but faces challenges
D) Space tourism is impossible
exercises.explanation_pt: O artigo descreve como o turismo espacial está se tornando realidade (lançamentos em 2021), mas menciona desafios (preço alto e preocupações ambientais).

exercises.passage: The Mediterranean diet, which emphasizes fruits, vegetables, whole grains, fish, and olive oil, has been consistently ranked as one of the healthiest diets in the world. A major study published in the New England Journal of Medicine found that people following a Mediterranean diet had a 30% lower risk of heart disease compared to those on a typical Western diet. The diet also appears to reduce the risk of type 2 diabetes and certain types of cancer.
exercises.question: By how much did the Mediterranean diet reduce heart disease risk?
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz '30% lower risk of heart disease' = 30% menor risco de doença cardíaca.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia e identifique o propósito do autor:
exercises.passage: Every year, millions of tons of plastic waste end up in our oceans. Marine animals mistake plastic for food, leading to injury and death. Microplastics have been found in drinking water, seafood, and even in the air we breathe. Despite growing awareness, global plastic production continues to increase. It is clear that individual action is not enough — we need systemic change through government regulation and corporate responsibility.
exercises.question: What is the author's purpose?
options: A) To argue that we need government and corporate action against plastic pollution
B) To explain how plastic is made
C) To promote plastic products
D) To entertain readers with a fun story
exercises.explanation_pt: O autor argumenta que ação individual não é suficiente e que precisamos de mudança sistêmica através de regulação governamental e responsabilidade corporativa.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia e identifique o tom do autor:
exercises.passage: The city council's decision to cut funding for public parks while approving a new luxury shopping mall is deeply disappointing. Parks provide essential green space for residents, improve air quality, and serve as gathering places for communities. Meanwhile, the city already has three shopping malls that struggle to fill their retail spaces. One has to wonder whose interests the council is truly serving.
exercises.question: What is the tone of this text?
options: A) Neutral and objective
B) Critical and disappointed
C) Happy and celebratory
D) Confused and uncertain
exercises.explanation_pt: O autor usa palavras como 'deeply disappointing' (profundamente decepcionante) e questiona os interesses do conselho, demonstrando tom crítico.

exercises.passage: Telemedicine — medical consultations conducted via video call — surged during the COVID-19 pandemic. Patients appreciated the convenience of seeing a doctor without leaving home, and many physicians found they could effectively diagnose and treat common conditions remotely. However, telemedicine has limitations: physical examinations are impossible, and patients in rural areas with poor internet connectivity may be excluded. As the pandemic subsides, many healthcare systems are adopting a hybrid approach, combining in-person and virtual visits.
exercises.question: What approach are healthcare systems adopting after the pandemic?
options: A) A hybrid of in-person and virtual visits
B) Only telemedicine
C) Eliminating all medical consultations
D) Only in-person visits
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que sistemas de saúde estão adotando uma abordagem híbrida (hybrid approach), combinando consultas presenciais e virtuais.

theme: Artigos sobre temas gerais — ideia principal + detalhes

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia o artigo e identifique a ideia principal e detalhes:
exercises.passage: The concept of 'smart cities' is gaining momentum around the world. Smart cities use technology and data to improve urban services, reduce waste, and enhance quality of life. For example, sensors can monitor traffic flow and adjust traffic lights in real time to reduce congestion. Smart waste bins signal when they need to be emptied, optimizing collection routes. Barcelona, Singapore, and Seoul are often cited as leading examples of smart city implementation.
exercises.question: What is the main purpose of smart city technology?
options: A) To make cities more expensive
B) To replace all human workers
C) To eliminate all traffic
D) To improve urban services and quality of life using data
exercises.explanation_pt: A ideia principal é que cidades inteligentes usam tecnologia e dados para melhorar serviços urbanos e qualidade de vida.

exercises.passage: The discovery of antibiotics in the 20th century was one of the greatest medical breakthroughs in history. Before antibiotics, simple infections could be fatal. However, the overuse and misuse of antibiotics has led to the emergence of antibiotic-resistant bacteria, sometimes called 'superbugs.' The World Health Organization has warned that antibiotic resistance is one of the biggest threats to global health. If new antibiotics are not developed and current ones are not used more responsibly, routine surgeries and minor infections could once again become life-threatening.
exercises.question: Why are 'superbugs' dangerous?
options: A) They are very large
B) They spread through the air
C) They cannot be killed by existing antibiotics
D) They only affect animals
exercises.explanation_pt: 'Superbugs' são bactérias resistentes a antibióticos — ou seja, os antibióticos existentes não conseguem matá-las.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia e identifique um detalhe específico:
exercises.passage: The James Webb Space Telescope, launched in December 2021, is the most powerful space telescope ever built. It orbits the Sun at a distance of 1.5 million kilometers from Earth, far beyond the Moon. The telescope uses infrared technology to observe the universe, allowing it to see through cosmic dust and detect light from the earliest galaxies, formed just a few hundred million years after the Big Bang. Scientists hope it will help answer fundamental questions about the origins of the universe.
exercises.question: How far from Earth does the James Webb Telescope orbit?
options: A) 150,000 kilometers
B) On the Moon
C) 1.5 million kilometers
D) 15 million kilometers
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz '1.5 million kilometers from Earth' = 1,5 milhão de quilômetros da Terra.

exercises.passage: Universal Basic Income (UBI) is a concept where every citizen receives a regular payment from the government, regardless of employment status. Proponents argue that UBI could eliminate poverty, reduce inequality, and provide a safety net in an era of increasing automation. Critics, however, question how it would be funded and worry that it might discourage people from working. Several countries have conducted pilot programs, with Finland's two-year experiment showing that UBI recipients reported greater happiness and well-being, though there was no significant increase in employment.
exercises.question: What did Finland's UBI experiment show?
options: A) Recipients were happier but didn't work more
B) The program was too expensive
C) People worked much more
D) People were unhappier
exercises.explanation_pt: O experimento da Finlândia mostrou que beneficiários reportaram maior felicidade (greater happiness), mas não houve aumento significativo no emprego.

exercises.passage: Microplastics — tiny plastic particles less than five millimeters in size — have been found in virtually every environment on Earth, from the deepest ocean trenches to the peak of Mount Everest. They enter the environment through the breakdown of larger plastic items, synthetic clothing fibers released during washing, and industrial processes. Recent studies have detected microplastics in human blood and lung tissue, raising urgent questions about their potential health effects. While research is still in its early stages, some scientists believe microplastics may cause inflammation, cellular damage, and hormonal disruption.
exercises.question: Where have microplastics been found?
options: A) Only in plastic factories
B) In virtually every environment, including human blood
C) Only in oceans
D) Only in cities
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que microplásticos foram encontrados em praticamente todo ambiente na Terra, incluindo sangue humano e tecido pulmonar.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia o texto acadêmico e responda:
exercises.passage: Cells are the basic building blocks of all living organisms. There are two main types of cells: prokaryotic and eukaryotic. Prokaryotic cells, found in bacteria, are simpler and smaller. They lack a nucleus and other membrane-bound organelles. Eukaryotic cells, found in plants, animals, and fungi, are larger and more complex. They have a nucleus that contains the cell's DNA, along with specialized organelles such as mitochondria and the endoplasmic reticulum.
exercises.question: What is the main difference between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells?
options: A) Eukaryotic cells lack a nucleus
B) There is no significant difference
C) Prokaryotic cells are larger
D) Prokaryotic cells lack a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que células procarióticas 'lack a nucleus and other membrane-bound organelles' = não possuem núcleo e outras organelas com membrana.

exercises.passage: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants convert sunlight into chemical energy. During this process, plants absorb carbon dioxide from the air and water from the soil. Using the energy from sunlight, they transform these raw materials into glucose, a type of sugar that serves as food for the plant. Oxygen is released as a byproduct of this reaction. Without photosynthesis, life on Earth as we know it would not be possible, as it is the primary source of oxygen in our atmosphere.
exercises.question: What is released as a byproduct of photosynthesis?
options: A) Water
B) Carbon dioxide
C) Glucose
D) Oxygen
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz 'Oxygen is released as a byproduct' = oxigênio é liberado como subproduto.

exercises.passage: The human immune system is a complex network of cells, tissues, and organs that work together to defend the body against harmful pathogens such as bacteria, viruses, and parasites. The first line of defense includes physical barriers like the skin and mucous membranes. If a pathogen manages to enter the body, the innate immune system responds quickly with white blood cells that attack the invader. If the infection persists, the adaptive immune system produces specialized antibodies that target the specific pathogen.
exercises.question: What does the adaptive immune system produce?
options: A) Mucous membranes
B) Physical barriers
C) Specialized antibodies
D) Red blood cells
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que o sistema imune adaptativo 'produces specialized antibodies' = produz anticorpos especializados.

exercises.passage: DNA, or deoxyribonucleic acid, carries the genetic instructions for the development, functioning, and reproduction of all known living organisms. The structure of DNA was famously described by James Watson and Francis Crick in 1953 as a double helix — two strands twisted around each other like a spiral staircase. Each strand is made up of nucleotides, which consist of a sugar, a phosphate group, and one of four nitrogenous bases: adenine (A), thymine (T), guanine (G), and cytosine (C).
exercises.question: How many nitrogenous bases make up DNA?
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto lista 4 bases nitrogenadas: adenina (A), timina (T), guanina (G) e citosina (C).

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia e identifique a ideia principal:
exercises.passage: Ecosystems are dynamic systems in which living organisms interact with each other and with their physical environment. Energy flows through ecosystems in a one-way direction, starting with producers (plants) that capture sunlight through photosynthesis. This energy is then transferred to primary consumers (herbivores), secondary consumers (carnivores), and eventually to decomposers. At each level of the food chain, approximately 90% of energy is lost as heat, which is why there are fewer top predators than herbivores in any ecosystem.
exercises.question: Why are there fewer top predators than herbivores?
options: A) Because herbivores are stronger
B) Because predators reproduce slowly
C) Because predators live shorter lives
D) Because 90% of energy is lost at each food chain level
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto explica que 90% da energia é perdida em cada nível da cadeia alimentar, por isso há menos predadores de topo.

exercises.passage: The Renaissance, which began in Italy in the 14th century and spread across Europe over the next three centuries, marked a period of profound cultural, artistic, and intellectual transformation. The word 'Renaissance' means 'rebirth' in French, reflecting the era's revival of interest in classical Greek and Roman culture. Key figures of the Renaissance include Leonardo da Vinci, Michelangelo, and Galileo Galilei. The movement emphasized humanism — the belief that human beings have great potential and that the study of the humanities (literature, philosophy, art) is valuable.
exercises.question: What does the word 'Renaissance' mean?
options: A) Recovery
B) Revolution
C) Reform
D) Rebirth
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz 'Renaissance means rebirth in French' = Renascimento significa 'renascimento' em francês.

exercises.passage: The French Revolution of 1789 was a watershed moment in world history. Sparked by widespread inequality, economic hardship, and resentment toward the monarchy, the revolution led to the overthrow of King Louis XVI and the establishment of a republic. The revolutionaries adopted the motto 'Liberté, Égalité, Fraternité' (Liberty, Equality, Fraternity), which remains the national motto of France today. However, the revolution also brought a period of extreme violence known as the Reign of Terror, during which thousands were executed by guillotine.
exercises.question: What was the Reign of Terror?
options: A) An economic reform program
B) A military campaign
C) A period of extreme violence with mass executions
D) A celebration of liberty
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto descreve o Reign of Terror como 'a period of extreme violence' durante o qual milhares foram executados.

exercises.passage: The ancient Egyptians built the Great Pyramids of Giza more than 4,500 years ago. The largest pyramid, known as the Great Pyramid or the Pyramid of Khufu, was the tallest man-made structure in the world for over 3,800 years. It is estimated that it took approximately 20 years and a workforce of around 20,000 to 30,000 laborers to complete. Contrary to popular belief, recent archaeological evidence suggests that the workers were not slaves but rather paid laborers who received food, housing, and medical care.
exercises.question: According to recent evidence, who built the pyramids?
options: A) Prisoners
B) Foreign workers
C) Slaves captured in war
D) Paid laborers who received food and housing
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que evidências recentes sugerem que os trabalhadores 'were not slaves but rather paid laborers' = não eram escravos, mas trabalhadores pagos.

exercises.passage: The Cold War, lasting from approximately 1947 to 1991, was a period of geopolitical tension between the United States and the Soviet Union. Although the two superpowers never engaged in direct military conflict, they competed through proxy wars, nuclear arms races, space exploration, and ideological propaganda. The Space Race was one of the most dramatic aspects of the Cold War, culminating in the American Apollo 11 mission, which successfully landed humans on the Moon in July 1969.
exercises.question: Did the US and Soviet Union fight each other directly?
options: A) Yes, in several major wars
B) Yes, but only once
C) The text doesn't mention this
D) No, they competed through proxy wars and other means
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que 'the two superpowers never engaged in direct military conflict' = nunca se enfrentaram diretamente, competindo por proxy wars.

exercises.passage: The abolition of slavery in Brazil did not happen overnight. While the Lei Áurea (Golden Law) of 1888 officially ended slavery, making Brazil the last country in the Western Hemisphere to do so, the process began decades earlier. The Lei do Ventre Livre (1871) freed children born to enslaved mothers, and the Lei dos Sexagenários (1885) freed enslaved people over 60. However, abolition did not come with any reparations or support for formerly enslaved people, who were left without land, education, or economic resources. Many historians argue that the legacy of this failure continues to shape Brazilian society today.
exercises.question: What can be inferred about the impact of abolition in Brazil?
options: A) It immediately solved all racial inequality
B) Formerly enslaved people received land and education
C) Brazil was the first country to abolish slavery
D) The lack of support after abolition created lasting social problems
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que não houve reparações ou suporte, e que historiadores argumentam que esse legado continua afetando a sociedade brasileira hoje.

exercises.passage: Abraham Maslow's hierarchy of needs is one of the most well-known theories in psychology. Maslow proposed that human needs are arranged in a pyramid, with the most basic needs at the bottom. Physiological needs (food, water, sleep) must be met first. Then come safety needs (security, stability), followed by social needs (love, belonging), esteem needs (respect, achievement), and finally self-actualization — the desire to reach one's full potential. According to Maslow, a person cannot focus on higher-level needs until the lower-level needs are satisfied.
exercises.question: According to Maslow, what must happen before a person can focus on self-actualization?
options: A) They must have a university degree
B) They must be wealthy
C) All lower-level needs must be satisfied first
D) They must be over 30 years old
exercises.explanation_pt: Maslow propôs que 'a person cannot focus on higher-level needs until the lower-level needs are satisfied' = precisa satisfazer necessidades inferiores primeiro.

exercises.passage: The Stanford Prison Experiment, conducted by Philip Zimbardo in 1971, is one of the most controversial studies in psychology. College students were randomly assigned to play either guards or prisoners in a simulated prison environment. Within days, the 'guards' began exhibiting authoritarian and abusive behavior, while the 'prisoners' showed signs of extreme stress and helplessness. The experiment was terminated after only six days — it was originally planned to last two weeks — due to the alarming psychological harm being inflicted on participants.
exercises.question: Why was the experiment stopped early?
options: A) Participants were suffering psychological harm
B) The prison was too small
C) The students got bored
D) The funding ran out
exercises.explanation_pt: O experimento foi encerrado 'due to the alarming psychological harm being inflicted on participants' = devido ao dano psicológico alarmante nos participantes.

exercises.passage: Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) is a widely used form of psychotherapy that focuses on identifying and changing negative thought patterns. The central idea behind CBT is that our thoughts influence our feelings, which in turn influence our behavior. For example, if someone thinks 'I am going to fail this exam,' they may feel anxious, which could lead to poor study habits and avoidance behavior. CBT teaches patients to recognize these automatic negative thoughts and replace them with more realistic and constructive ones.
exercises.question: What is the central idea of CBT?
options: A) Our thoughts influence our feelings and behavior
B) Dreams reveal our subconscious desires
C) Childhood experiences determine adult behavior
D) Medication is the best treatment
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que a ideia central da TCC é que 'our thoughts influence our feelings, which in turn influence our behavior'.

exercises.passage: The concept of emotional intelligence (EI), popularized by psychologist Daniel Goleman in 1995, refers to the ability to recognize, understand, manage, and effectively use emotions — both one's own and those of others. Research has shown that emotional intelligence is often a better predictor of professional success than IQ. People with high EI tend to have stronger relationships, handle stress more effectively, and demonstrate better leadership skills. Unlike IQ, which is relatively fixed, emotional intelligence can be developed and improved throughout life.
exercises.question: How does emotional intelligence compare to IQ in terms of changeability?
options: A) Neither can be measured accurately
B) IQ can be changed but EI cannot
C) Both are completely fixed
D) EI can be developed throughout life, unlike IQ which is relatively fixed
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz 'Unlike IQ, which is relatively fixed, emotional intelligence can be developed and improved throughout life'.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia e identifique o propósito do autor:
exercises.passage: The growth mindset theory, developed by Carol Dweck, suggests that intelligence and abilities are not fixed traits but can be developed through effort, good strategies, and input from others. Students with a growth mindset embrace challenges, persist in the face of setbacks, and view failure as an opportunity to learn. In contrast, students with a fixed mindset believe that intelligence is innate and unchangeable, leading them to avoid challenges and give up easily when faced with difficulty. Dweck's research has had a profound impact on education, encouraging teachers to praise effort rather than innate ability.
exercises.question: What does a growth mindset encourage students to do?
options: A) Embrace challenges and view failure as a learning opportunity
B) Believe intelligence is fixed
C) Compete against other students
D) Avoid difficult tasks
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que alunos com growth mindset 'embrace challenges' e 'view failure as an opportunity to learn'.

exercises.passage: Artificial intelligence (AI) refers to computer systems designed to perform tasks that typically require human intelligence, such as visual perception, speech recognition, decision-making, and language translation. Machine learning, a subset of AI, enables computers to learn from data without being explicitly programmed. Deep learning, a further subset, uses neural networks with many layers to analyze complex patterns. While AI has made remarkable advances in fields like healthcare, transportation, and communication, critics raise concerns about job displacement, algorithmic bias, and the ethical implications of autonomous decision-making.
exercises.question: What is the relationship between AI, machine learning, and deep learning?
options: A) They are competing technologies
B) Machine learning is a subset of AI, and deep learning is a subset of machine learning
C) They are the same thing
D) AI is a subset of machine learning
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto descreve: AI é o conceito geral, machine learning é um subconjunto de AI, e deep learning é um subconjunto de machine learning.

exercises.passage: CRISPR-Cas9 is a revolutionary gene-editing technology that allows scientists to modify DNA with unprecedented precision. Discovered in bacteria, where it serves as a natural defense against viruses, CRISPR has been adapted for use in medical research, agriculture, and biotechnology. Potential applications include correcting genetic mutations that cause diseases like sickle cell anemia, creating drought-resistant crops, and even eliminating malaria-carrying mosquitoes. However, the technology raises profound ethical questions, particularly regarding the possibility of editing human embryos to create so-called 'designer babies.'
exercises.question: What ethical concern does the text mention about CRISPR?
options: A) It could be used to create 'designer babies'
B) It is too expensive
C) It causes cancer
D) It only works on plants
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto menciona preocupações éticas sobre 'editing human embryos to create designer babies' = editar embriões humanos para criar 'bebês projetados'.

exercises.passage: Quantum computing represents a fundamentally different approach to computation compared to classical computers. While classical computers use bits that can be either 0 or 1, quantum computers use quantum bits, or 'qubits,' which can exist in multiple states simultaneously thanks to a property called superposition. This allows quantum computers to process vast amounts of information much faster than traditional computers for certain types of problems. However, quantum computers are extremely sensitive to environmental interference and currently require near-absolute-zero temperatures to operate.
exercises.question: What makes qubits different from regular bits?
options: A) Qubits can only be 0
B) Qubits are faster
C) Qubits are cheaper to produce
D) Qubits can exist in multiple states simultaneously
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que qubits 'can exist in multiple states simultaneously thanks to superposition' = podem existir em múltiplos estados simultaneamente.

exercises.passage: The Internet of Things (IoT) refers to the growing network of physical devices connected to the internet that can collect and exchange data. From smart thermostats and fitness trackers to connected cars and industrial sensors, IoT devices are transforming how we live and work. By 2030, it is estimated that there will be over 25 billion IoT devices worldwide. While IoT offers tremendous benefits in terms of efficiency and convenience, it also creates significant cybersecurity risks, as each connected device represents a potential entry point for hackers.
exercises.question: What is a major risk of IoT devices?
options: A) Each device is a potential entry point for hackers
B) They use too much electricity
C) They don't work without Wi-Fi
D) They are too expensive for consumers
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que cada dispositivo conectado 'represents a potential entry point for hackers' = representa um ponto de entrada potencial para hackers.

exercises.passage: Blockchain technology, originally developed as the underlying system for Bitcoin, is a decentralized digital ledger that records transactions across a network of computers. Each block in the chain contains a group of transactions, and once a block is added to the chain, it cannot be altered without changing all subsequent blocks — making the system highly resistant to tampering. Beyond cryptocurrency, blockchain has potential applications in supply chain management, voting systems, healthcare records, and intellectual property protection.
exercises.question: Why is blockchain resistant to tampering?
options: A) Changing one block requires changing all subsequent blocks
B) It is stored on a single secure server
C) Only one person can access it
D) It uses strong passwords
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto explica que 'once a block is added, it cannot be altered without changing all subsequent blocks' = para alterar um bloco, todos os blocos subsequentes precisariam ser alterados.

exercises.passage: Ocean acidification, often called 'the other CO2 problem,' occurs when the ocean absorbs excess carbon dioxide from the atmosphere. The absorbed CO2 reacts with seawater to form carbonic acid, lowering the ocean's pH. Since the Industrial Revolution, ocean acidity has increased by approximately 30%. This change threatens marine organisms that build shells or skeletons from calcium carbonate, such as corals, oysters, and certain species of plankton. As these organisms struggle to survive, the entire marine food web could be disrupted, with far-reaching consequences for both ocean ecosystems and human communities that depend on seafood.
exercises.question: By how much has ocean acidity increased since the Industrial Revolution?
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz 'ocean acidity has increased by approximately 30%' = a acidez oceânica aumentou aproximadamente 30%.

exercises.passage: The concept of a circular economy represents a fundamental shift from the traditional 'take-make-dispose' linear economic model. In a circular economy, products and materials are designed to be reused, repaired, refurbished, and recycled, minimizing waste and reducing the extraction of new raw materials. Companies like Patagonia, which repairs and resells used clothing, and Interface, which recycles old carpets into new ones, are leading examples of this approach. Proponents argue that a circular economy can reduce environmental impact while creating new business opportunities and jobs.
exercises.question: What is the main difference between a linear and circular economy?
options: A) Circular only works for clothing
B) Circular focuses on reuse and recycling instead of disposal
C) Linear is cheaper
D) Linear uses more technology
exercises.explanation_pt: A economia circular foca em reusar, reparar e reciclar em vez do modelo linear de 'take-make-dispose' (extrair-produzir-descartar).

exercises.passage: Rewilding is a conservation strategy that aims to restore ecosystems by reintroducing species that have been lost from an area. One of the most famous examples is the reintroduction of wolves to Yellowstone National Park in 1995. The wolves controlled the elk population, which had been overgrazing along riverbanks. With fewer elk, vegetation recovered, stabilizing riverbanks and reducing erosion. This in turn created better habitats for fish, birds, and beavers. This cascade of ecological effects, triggered by the return of a single predator, is known as a trophic cascade.
exercises.question: What is a trophic cascade?
options: A) A disease that affects wolves
B) A type of waterfall
C) A chain of ecological effects triggered by changes at one level of the food chain
D) A method of farming
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto descreve a cascata trófica como uma cadeia de efeitos ecológicos desencadeada pela mudança em um nível da cadeia alimentar (no caso, a reintrodução dos lobos).

exercises.passage: Urban heat islands occur when cities experience significantly higher temperatures than surrounding rural areas. Several factors contribute to this phenomenon: dark-colored surfaces like asphalt and rooftops absorb more heat than natural landscapes; tall buildings trap heat and block cooling winds; air conditioning units release waste heat into the streets; and the lack of vegetation means less cooling through evapotranspiration. Studies show that urban heat islands can increase city temperatures by 1-3°C on average, and up to 12°C at night. This effect disproportionately impacts low-income neighborhoods, which often have fewer trees and more pavement.
exercises.question: Who is most affected by urban heat islands?
options: A) Rural communities
B) Suburban areas
C) Low-income neighborhoods
D) Wealthy neighborhoods
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que o efeito 'disproportionately impacts low-income neighborhoods' = afeta desproporcionalmente bairros de baixa renda.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia e identifique o argumento principal:
exercises.passage: While many environmentalists advocate for individual actions like recycling and using reusable bags, a growing number of scientists and activists argue that systemic change is far more important. According to a widely cited report, just 100 companies are responsible for 71% of global greenhouse gas emissions. Individual consumer choices, while symbolically important, cannot offset the environmental damage caused by industrial-scale fossil fuel extraction, deforestation, and manufacturing. Critics of the individual-action approach argue that corporations have deliberately promoted the idea of personal responsibility to deflect attention from their own practices.
exercises.question: What is the author's main argument?
options: A) Systemic change at the corporate/industrial level is more important than individual actions
B) Individual actions are pointless
C) Recycling is the most important thing individuals can do
D) Companies are already doing enough
exercises.explanation_pt: O argumento principal é que mudanças sistêmicas no nível corporativo/industrial são mais importantes que ações individuais, já que 100 empresas são responsáveis por 71% das emissões.

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia o texto acadêmico e responda:
exercises.passage: Globalization has created an increasingly interconnected world economy, but its benefits have not been distributed equally. While multinational corporations and wealthy nations have prospered from free trade and open markets, many developing countries have seen their local industries decimated by competition from cheaper imports. Critics argue that globalization has widened the gap between rich and poor nations, created a 'race to the bottom' in labor standards and environmental regulations, and concentrated economic power in the hands of a few multinational corporations. Proponents counter that globalization has lifted hundreds of millions of people out of extreme poverty, particularly in China and India.
exercises.question: According to critics, what has globalization done to the gap between rich and poor?
options: A) Reduced it slightly
B) Widened it
C) Eliminated it
D) Had no effect
exercises.explanation_pt: Críticos argumentam que a globalização 'has widened the gap between rich and poor nations' = ampliou a diferença entre nações ricas e pobres.

exercises.passage: Universal healthcare systems, in which the government provides medical coverage to all citizens regardless of income, exist in most developed countries. Canada, the United Kingdom, and Australia all have some form of universal healthcare. The United States remains the only developed nation without such a system, relying instead on a mix of private insurance, employer-sponsored plans, and government programs like Medicare and Medicaid. Supporters of universal healthcare argue that access to medical care is a fundamental human right and that universal systems reduce administrative costs. Opponents contend that government-run healthcare leads to longer wait times, reduced quality, and higher taxes.
exercises.question: Which country does NOT have universal healthcare according to the text?
options: A) Canada
B) Australia
C) United States
D) United Kingdom
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz que os EUA 'remains the only developed nation without such a system' = continua sendo a única nação desenvolvida sem esse sistema.

exercises.passage: The gig economy — characterized by short-term contracts, freelance work, and app-based services like Uber and DoorDash — has fundamentally changed the nature of employment. Gig workers enjoy flexibility and autonomy, choosing when and how much they work. However, they typically lack the benefits that traditional employees receive: health insurance, paid vacation, retirement plans, and job security. Labor advocates argue that gig companies have deliberately misclassified workers as 'independent contractors' to avoid providing these benefits, effectively creating a two-tier labor market where a growing number of workers have fewer protections.
exercises.question: Why do labor advocates say gig companies classify workers as independent contractors?
options: A) To avoid providing benefits like health insurance
B) To give workers more freedom
C) Because workers prefer this classification
D) Because the law requires it
exercises.explanation_pt: Defensores dos trabalhadores argumentam que empresas classificam como 'independent contractors' para 'avoid providing these benefits' = evitar fornecer benefícios.

exercises.passage: Inflation occurs when the general price level of goods and services in an economy rises over time, reducing the purchasing power of money. Moderate inflation (2-3% annually) is generally considered healthy for an economy, as it encourages spending and investment. However, high inflation can be devastating: wages fail to keep up with prices, savings lose their value, and economic uncertainty increases. Central banks, such as the Federal Reserve in the United States and the Banco Central do Brasil, use monetary policy tools — primarily adjusting interest rates — to control inflation. When inflation is too high, central banks raise interest rates to slow economic activity and reduce price pressures.
exercises.question: What do central banks do when inflation is too high?
options: A) Print more money
B) Raise interest rates
C) Increase government spending
D) Lower interest rates
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto diz 'When inflation is too high, central banks raise interest rates' = quando a inflação é alta, bancos centrais aumentam as taxas de juros.

exercises.passage: The concept of 'food deserts' refers to urban areas where residents have limited access to affordable, nutritious food. These neighborhoods typically lack supermarkets and grocery stores, forcing residents to rely on convenience stores and fast-food restaurants that offer primarily processed, high-calorie foods. Food deserts disproportionately affect low-income communities and communities of color. Research has linked food deserts to higher rates of obesity, diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. Solutions proposed include incentivizing supermarkets to open in underserved areas, supporting community gardens, and improving public transportation to enable access to existing grocery stores.
exercises.question: What can be inferred about the relationship between food deserts and health?
options: A) Limited access to nutritious food contributes to higher disease rates
B) Food deserts only affect rural areas
C) People in food deserts are healthier because they eat less
D) Food deserts have no impact on health
exercises.explanation_pt: O texto liga food deserts a taxas maiores de obesidade, diabetes e doenças cardiovasculares, inferindo que acesso limitado a comida nutritiva contribui para mais doenças.

exercises.passage: The Sapir-Whorf hypothesis, also known as linguistic relativity, suggests that the language we speak influences the way we think and perceive the world. The strong version of this hypothesis — that language determines thought — has been largely discredited. However, the weak version — that language influences certain aspects of thought — has received considerable empirical support. For example, speakers of languages that have separate words for light blue and dark blue (like Russian) can distinguish between these shades faster than English speakers, who use a single word 'blue' for both.
exercises.question: What does the weak version of the Sapir-Whorf hypothesis claim?
options: A) Language completely determines thought
B) Language influences certain aspects of thought
C) Language has no effect on thought
D) All languages are identical
exercises.explanation_pt: A versão fraca diz que 'language influences certain aspects of thought' = a linguagem influencia certos aspectos do pensamento.

exercises.passage: Code-switching — the practice of alternating between two or more languages within a conversation — is a common phenomenon among bilingual and multilingual speakers.
